title: Urban Atlas-based U-Space corridor network for a pilot city
summary: A CAA used CLMS Urban Atlas to derive a 3D obstacle model and design a low-altitude drone corridor network.
narrative:
## Challenge
Developing a U-Space implementation, the authority needed detailed, consistent land-use and building-morphology data to define corridors, exclusion zones and contingency landing areas. Existing cadastral data was outdated and inconsistent.

## Approach
CLMS Urban Atlas provided harmonised land-use classification across the city. Combined with building-height data, it produced a 3D obstacle model and a ground-risk grid, used to generate a candidate corridor network for the U-Space volume.

## Results
The corridor network was approved for the pilot U-Space implementation. The Urban Atlas-based methodology was highlighted as a replicable approach for ground-risk mapping in mid-sized European cities.
